title: K12Orgs
text:
This dataset is of all the individuals that have graduated from a high school in Southwest Minnesota. While preparing the K12Enrollment data, I already joined the organizational data and created a dataset that has all the individuals that graduated from a school located in EDR 6E, 6W and 8.

The one metric that wasn’t included when I joined the enrollment data with the organizational data is the number of years they attended a school in the Southwest region.


enrollment.original <-read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/CRPD 2 (angelique.workman@state.mn.us)/K12Enrollment.csv")  %>%
  mutate(PersonID = as.integer(PersonID),
         K12OrganizationID = as.integer(K12OrganizationID)) %>%
  drop_na(PersonID)

SiteCountyCode <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/SiteCountyCodes.csv")

k12.org.original <- read_csv('/Users/kellyasche/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/CRPD 2 (angelique.workman@state.mn.us)/K12Organization_cleaned.csv') %>%
  distinct(K12OrganizationID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  drop_na(K12OrganizationID) %>%
  mutate(K12OrganizationID = as.integer(K12OrganizationID)) %>%
  group_by(NCESID) %>%
  fill(SiteCountyCode, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup()
  

k12.org.master <- k12.org.original %>%
  left_join(SiteCountyCode[,c(1,2)], by = c("SiteCountyCode" = "Code")) %>%
  drop_na(SiteCountyCode) %>%
  rename(county.name = `Code definition`) %>%
  select(K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, county.name) %>%
  mutate(county.name = str_replace(county.name, " County", ""),
         county.name = str_replace(county.name, "Saint Louis", "St. Louis")) %>%
  filter(county.name != "Unknown") %>%
  select(K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, county.name) %>%
  left_join(counties.regions.1, by = c("county.name" = "Name")) %>%
  select(-Dem_RUCA, -mif, -edr.simple)

enrollment.org.ne <- enrollment.original %>%
  left_join(k12.org.master, by = "K12OrganizationID") %>%
  filter(project.pr == "Southwest")

n.years <- original %>%
  select(PersonID, grad.year, K12OrganizationID) %>%
  rename(grad.org.id = K12OrganizationID) %>%
  left_join(enrollment.original[,c(1,2,3,4)], by = "PersonID") %>%
  left_join(k12.org.original[,c(1,2,3,7)], by = "K12OrganizationID") %>%
  mutate(year = str_sub(TimeID, 1, 4)) %>%
  distinct(PersonID, year, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  summarize(n.years.attended = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

master <- original %>%
  left_join(n.years, by = "PersonID")

kable(head(master))

title: K12Orgs
text:
Summary of high school location
Lets breakdown the graduates by which RUCA category their high school was located.
Nearly 71% of the observations graduated from a county categorized as “town/rural mix”, followed by 15.1% that graduated from a high school in urban/town/rural mix counties and 14% from entirely rural counties.








Below is a map with the percentages of graduates graduating from high schools located within each county.

title: K12Orgs
text:
Summary of student demographics
The following analysis looks to see if there are any statistical differences due to rural-ness and geographic location of the high school for the following graduate demographics;

Were eligible for free or reduced lunch at some point in the dataset
Are Female
Have been homeless at some point in the dataset
Had limited english proficiency at some point in the dataset
Had non english speakders in home at some point in the dataset
Participated in a PSEO course at some point in the dataset.

The cross-tabs below indicate that there are 4 demographic variables that have proportions statistically different across RUCA categories. Each of the variables are indicated with an asterisk.

Free or reduced lunch: the more rural a district is, the higher the percentage of graduates that qualified for free or reduced lunch.
Homeless: as a school district becomes more urban, the percentage of it’s graduates indicating they experienced homelessness at some point increases significantly.
Limited English: the entirely rural districts had a slightly higher percentage of graduates with limited English.
Non-English in home: town/rural mix districts had a significantly higher percentage of graduates with non-English speakers in the home.
PSEO: a significantly lower percentage of graduates from entirely rural districts participated in a PSEO program.


RUCA Free or Reduced LunchRUCA FemaleRUCA HomelessRUCA Limited EnglishRUCA non-english in homeRUCA PSEO


Although 36.8% of the total number of graduates in the dataset receive free or reduced lunch, the more rural school districts had a higher percentage - 45.1% for entirely rural and 40.1% for town/rural mix schools.


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$economic.status, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  54063 

 
                     | master$economic.status 
     master$Dem_Desc |         0 |         1 | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      5181 |      2476 |      7657 | 
                     |  4815.598 |  2841.402 |           | 
                     |     0.677 |     0.323 |     0.142 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     22681 |     15559 |     38240 | 
                     | 24049.687 | 14190.313 |           | 
                     |     0.593 |     0.407 |     0.707 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      6139 |      2027 |      8166 | 
                     |  5135.715 |  3030.285 |           | 
                     |     0.752 |     0.248 |     0.151 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     34001 |     20062 |     54063 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  812.7926     d.f. =  2     p =  3.19396e-177 


 




k12.dem.free.lunch.ruca <- master %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
           free.lunch = sum(economic.status)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(pct = free.lunch / n,
         total.pct = sum(free.lunch) / sum(n),
         data_id = as.character(seq(n()))) 

k12.dem.free.lunch.ruca.plot <- ggplot(k12.dem.free.lunch.ruca, aes(Dem_Desc, pct, fill = Dem_Desc)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = k12.dem.free.lunch.ruca$total.pct, color = "black") +
  geom_col_interactive(size = 5, aes(data_id = data_id, tooltip = paste(Dem_Desc, "\nTotal graduates: ", comma(n, accuracy = 1), "\nGraduates received free or reduced lunch: ", comma(free.lunch, accuracy = 1), "\nPercent of total graduates: ", percent(pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""))) +
  annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 3,
           y = .4,
           label = "All graduates\npercent, 36.8%",
           size = 5) +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", fill = "", title = "Percent of graduates receiving free or reduced lunch")+
  geom_label(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = .1)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent)+
  theme_line+
  scale_fill_manual(values= color.ruca,
                     guide = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 16))

  girafe(ggobj = k12.dem.free.lunch.ruca.plot) %>%
    girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                   opts_toolbar(saveaspng = FALSE),
                   opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE))








No significance.


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$Gender, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  54063 

 
                     | master$Gender 
     master$Dem_Desc |         F |         M | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      3834 |      3823 |      7657 | 
                     |  3771.069 |  3885.931 |           | 
                     |     0.501 |     0.499 |     0.142 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     18739 |     19501 |     38240 | 
                     | 18833.181 | 19406.819 |           | 
                     |     0.490 |     0.510 |     0.707 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      4053 |      4113 |      8166 | 
                     |  4021.751 |  4144.249 |           | 
                     |     0.496 |     0.504 |     0.151 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     26626 |     27437 |     54063 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  3.475817     d.f. =  2     p =  0.1758879 


 





As a district becomes more urban, the percentage of it’s graduates indicating it experienced homelessness increases significantly.


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$HomelessIndicator, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  54063 

 
                     | master$HomelessIndicator 
     master$Dem_Desc |         N |         Y | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      7632 |        25 |      7657 | 
                     |  7619.751 |    37.249 |           | 
                     |     0.997 |     0.003 |     0.142 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     38038 |       202 |     38240 | 
                     | 38053.974 |   186.026 |           | 
                     |     0.995 |     0.005 |     0.707 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      8130 |        36 |      8166 | 
                     |  8126.275 |    39.725 |           | 
                     |     0.996 |     0.004 |     0.151 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     53800 |       263 |     54063 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  5.777065     d.f. =  2     p =  0.05565783 


 



k12.dem.homeless.ruca <- master %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc, HomelessIndicator) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(total.pct = sum(n[HomelessIndicator == "Y"]) / sum(n),
         graduates = sum(n)) %>%
  filter(HomelessIndicator == "Y") %>%
  mutate(total.homeless = sum(n),
         data_id = seq(n()))

  
k12.dem.homeless.ruca.plot <- ggplot(k12.dem.homeless.ruca, aes(Dem_Desc, pct, fill = Dem_Desc)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = k12.dem.homeless.ruca$total.pct, color = "black") +
  geom_col_interactive(size = 5, aes(data_id = data_id, tooltip = paste(Dem_Desc, "\nTotal graduates: ", comma(graduates, accuracy = 1), "\nGraduates homeless at some point: ", comma(total.homeless, accuracy = 1), "\nPercent of total graduates: ", percent(pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""))) +
  annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 1,
           y = k12.dem.homeless.ruca$total.pct - .001,
           label = paste("All graduates\npercent, ", percent(k12.dem.homeless.ruca$total.pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""),
           size = 5) +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", fill = "", title = "Percent of graduates homeless")+
  geom_label(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = .1)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent)+
  theme_line+
  scale_fill_manual(values= color.ruca,
                     guide = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 16))

  girafe(ggobj = k12.dem.homeless.ruca.plot) %>%
    girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                   opts_toolbar(saveaspng = FALSE),
                   opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE))








The cross-tabs indicate a relationship. The chart shows that our .3% of entirely rural graduates were tagged as having Limited English which is significantly higher than town/rural mix (.1%).


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  54063 

 
                     | master$LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator 
     master$Dem_Desc |         N |         Y | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      7546 |       111 |      7657 | 
                     |  7432.090 |   224.910 |           | 
                     |     0.986 |     0.014 |     0.142 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     36842 |      1398 |     38240 | 
                     | 37116.771 |  1123.229 |           | 
                     |     0.963 |     0.037 |     0.707 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      8087 |        79 |      8166 | 
                     |  7926.139 |   239.861 |           | 
                     |     0.990 |     0.010 |     0.151 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     52475 |      1588 |     54063 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  239.8332     d.f. =  2     p =  8.334709e-53 


 



k12.dem.limited.english.ruca <- master %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc, LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(total.pct = sum(n[LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator == "Y"]) / sum(n),
         graduates = sum(n)) %>%
  filter(LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator == "Y") %>%
  mutate(LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator.total = sum(n),
         data_id = seq(n()))

  
k12.dem.limited.english.ruca.plot <- ggplot(k12.dem.limited.english.ruca, aes(Dem_Desc, pct, fill = Dem_Desc)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = k12.dem.limited.english.ruca$total.pct, color = "black") +
  geom_col_interactive(size = 5, aes(data_id = data_id, tooltip = paste(Dem_Desc, "\nTotal graduates: ", comma(graduates, accuracy = 1), "\nGraduates homeless at some point: ", comma(LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator.total, accuracy = 1), "\nPercent of total graduates: ", percent(pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""))) +
  annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 2,
           y = k12.dem.limited.english.ruca$total.pct + .0005,
           label = paste("All graduates\npercent, ", percent(k12.dem.limited.english.ruca$total.pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""),
           size = 5) +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", fill = "", title = "Percent of graduates with Limited English")+
  geom_label(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = .1)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent)+
  theme_line+
  scale_fill_manual(values= color.ruca,
                     guide = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 16))

  girafe(ggobj = k12.dem.limited.english.ruca.plot) %>%
    girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                   opts_toolbar(saveaspng = FALSE),
                   opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE))








Okay, well this doesn’t make a ton of sense. In the previous chart it showed that town/rural mix graduates were significantly less likely to have limited english, while this shows that town/rural mix graduates have the highest percentage of people that had non-english speakers at home.


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$non.english.home, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  54063 

 
                     | master$non.english.home 
     master$Dem_Desc |         0 |         1 | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      7333 |       324 |      7657 | 
                     |  6888.510 |   768.490 |           | 
                     |     0.958 |     0.042 |     0.142 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     33471 |      4769 |     38240 | 
                     | 34402.066 |  3837.934 |           | 
                     |     0.875 |     0.125 |     0.707 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      7833 |       333 |      8166 | 
                     |  7346.424 |   819.576 |           | 
                     |     0.959 |     0.041 |     0.151 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     48637 |      5426 |     54063 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  857.9462     d.f. =  2     p =  5.004327e-187 


 




k12.non.english.home.ruca <- master %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc) %>%
  summarise(n = n(),
            non.english = sum(non.english.home)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(pct = non.english / n,
         total.pct = sum(non.english) / sum(n),
         data_id = seq(n()))
  
k12.non.english.home.ruca.plot <- ggplot(k12.non.english.home.ruca, aes(Dem_Desc, pct, fill = Dem_Desc)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = k12.non.english.home.ruca$total.pct, color = "black") +
  geom_col_interactive(size = 5, aes(data_id = data_id, tooltip = paste(Dem_Desc, "\nTotal graduates: ", comma(n, accuracy = 1), "\nGraduates with non-english speaker\nat home: ", comma(non.english, accuracy = 1), "\nPercent of total graduates: ", percent(pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""))) +
  annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 1,
           y = k12.non.english.home.ruca$total.pct + .001,
           label = paste("All graduates\npercent, ", percent(k12.non.english.home.ruca$total.pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""),
           size = 5) +
  labs(x="", y = "", fill = "", color="", title = "Percent of graduates with non-english\n speaker at home")+
  geom_label(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = .1)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent)+
  theme_line+
  scale_fill_manual(values= color.ruca,
                     guide = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 16))

  girafe(ggobj = k12.non.english.home.ruca.plot) %>%
    girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                   opts_toolbar(saveaspng = FALSE),
                   opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE))








Although 38.4% of all graduates participating in PSEO, there was a significantly lower percentage in entirely rural schools (30%).


CrossTable(master$Dem_Desc, master$pseo.participant, expected = TRUE, prop.t = FALSE, prop.c = FALSE, prop.chisq = FALSE)


 
   Cell Contents
|-------------------------|
|                       N |
|              Expected N |
|           N / Row Total |
|-------------------------|

 
Total Observations in Table:  43776 

 
                     | master$pseo.participant 
     master$Dem_Desc |         0 |         1 | Row Total | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Entirely rural |      3557 |      2428 |      5985 | 
                     |  3512.578 |  2472.422 |           | 
                     |     0.594 |     0.406 |     0.137 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
      Town/rural mix |     18642 |     12482 |     31124 | 
                     | 18266.580 | 12857.420 |           | 
                     |     0.599 |     0.401 |     0.711 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
Urban/town/rural mix |      3493 |      3174 |      6667 | 
                     |  3912.842 |  2754.158 |           | 
                     |     0.524 |     0.476 |     0.152 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
        Column Total |     25692 |     18084 |     43776 | 
---------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|

 
Statistics for All Table Factors


Pearson's Chi-squared test 
------------------------------------------------------------
Chi^2 =  129.0862     d.f. =  2     p =  9.317381e-29 


 




k12.pseo.ruca<- master %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc, pseo.participant) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(total.graduates = sum(n)) %>%
  group_by(Dem_Desc) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n),
         total.graduates = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(pseo.participant = ifelse(is.na(pseo.participant), -1, pseo.participant),
         total.pct = sum(n[pseo.participant == 1]) / sum(n)) %>%
  filter(pseo.participant == 1) %>%
  mutate(data_id = seq(n()))

k12.pseo.ruca.plot <- ggplot(k12.pseo.ruca, aes(Dem_Desc, pct, fill = Dem_Desc)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = k12.pseo.ruca$total.pct, color = "black") +
  geom_col_interactive(size = 5, aes(data_id = data_id, tooltip = paste(Dem_Desc, "\nTotal graduates: ", comma(total.graduates, accuracy = 1), "\nGraduates participated in PSEO: ", comma(n, accuracy = 1), "\nPercent of total graduates: ", percent(pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""))) +
  annotate(geom = "text",
           x = 1,
           y = k12.pseo.ruca$total.pct,
           label = paste("All graduates\npercent, ", percent(k12.pseo.ruca$total.pct, accuracy = .1), sep = ""),
           size = 5) +
  labs(x="", y = "", color="", fill = "", title = "Percent of graduates participated in PSEO")+
  geom_label(aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = .1)), show.legend = FALSE, size = 5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent)+
  theme_line+
  scale_fill_manual(values= color.ruca,
                     guide = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        text = element_text(size = 16))

  girafe(ggobj = k12.pseo.ruca.plot) %>%
    girafe_options(opts_selection(type = "none"),
                   opts_toolbar(saveaspng = FALSE),
                   opts_sizing(rescale = FALSE))

title: K12Enrollment

Data prep
This dataset is of all the individuals that have graduated from a high school in Northeast Minnesota.


enrollment.original <- read_csv('/Users/kellyasche/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/CRPD 2 (angelique.workman@state.mn.us)/K12Enrollment.csv')  %>%
  mutate(PersonID = as.integer(PersonID),
         K12OrganizationID = as.integer(K12OrganizationID)) %>%
  drop_na(PersonID)

In the dataset there are 2,121,377 observations along with 15 columns. Each observation does not represent a unique, unduplicated individual, but rather a unique status for the individual. For example, one individual can have multiple observations if they changed schools, for each school year, or if their economic status changed in the middle of a school year.
Next we need to create a dataset that only has students who graduated from a high school located in the Southwest research region - EDRs 6W, 6E, and 8. To do that, we will need to join up with the organization spreadsheet.


k12.org.original <- read_csv('/Users/kellyasche/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/CRPD 2 (angelique.workman@state.mn.us)/K12Organization_cleaned.csv') %>%
  distinct(K12OrganizationID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  drop_na(K12OrganizationID) %>%
  mutate(K12OrganizationID = as.integer(K12OrganizationID)) %>%
  group_by(NCESID) %>%
  fill(SiteCountyCode, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup()
  
kable(head(k12.org.original))

This dataset provides each school institution as of June 29, 2024. This isn’t a list of each school but rather a list of each K12OrganizationID. Each school will have multiple K12OrganizationID. I’m not sure why and this would be something to ask Meredith.
In addition, there are a lot of county codes missing. So I’m going to use the listed city to try and clean that up.
My goal is to join my standardized region and county code data with this document which will then be joined to the master enrollment document. Unfortunately the main data does not include county name but rather their own code for county. So I will join their codes with the county names and then use my counties.regions data to bring together the remaining regions.


SiteCountyCode <- read_csv("~/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/SiteCountyCodes.csv")

k12.org.master <- k12.org.original %>%
  left_join(SiteCountyCode[,c(1,2)], by = c("SiteCountyCode" = "Code")) %>%
  drop_na(SiteCountyCode) %>%
  rename(county.name = `Code definition`) %>%
  select(K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, county.name) %>%
  mutate(county.name = str_replace(county.name, " County", ""),
         county.name = str_replace(county.name, "Saint Louis", "St. Louis")) %>%
  filter(county.name != "Unknown") %>%
  select(K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, county.name) %>%
  left_join(counties.regions.1, by = c("county.name" = "Name")) %>%
  select(-Dem_RUCA, -mif, -edr.simple)

Now I should be able to join this dataset with the enrollment data to figure out what EDR they are from and then filter any rows that are not associated with a school district located in EDR 3.

  left_join(k12.org.master, by = "K12OrganizationID") %>%
  select(-statefp) %>%
  filter(planning.region == "Southwest")

PersonID


K12OrganizationID


TimeID


Grade


EconomicStatusIndicator


FederalRaceIndicator


PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator


PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator


RaceEthnicity


StatusEnd


Gender


SpecialEdStatus


LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator


HomePrimaryLanguage


HomelessIndicator


OrganizationName


county.name


countyfp


Dem_Desc


edr


planning.region






The dataset now has 216,240 instances that provide a number of characteristics about an individuals enrollment.
My goal is to end up with a dataset where each observation (row) represents a unique (unduplicated) individual that’s tied to the school from which they graduated. Here’s how I will do that.

PersonID: change to integer
K12OrganizationID: change to integer.
K12EnrTimeID: Change so it’s only the year
Grade: eliminate - unimportant since we are mostly concerned about whether they graduated.
EconomicStatusIndicator: I will change this to two categories. Whether they were ever eligible for reduce priced or free meal will be coded as 1 (eligible = codes 1, 2, 4, 5) at any point in the dataset. The remaining will be coded as 0.
FederalRaceIndicator: eliminate - I’m going to use the RaceEthnicity column instead.
PSEO columns: I’m most concerned about whether they participated in PSEO or not. A new column will be created indicated whether there was a “Y” coded for either the “PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator” or “PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator” columns at any point in the dataset. The two original columns will be eliminated.
StatusEnd: I will only include individuals that have StatusEnd coded as an 8 or 9.
Gender: included and not changed.
SpecialEdStatus: I will split this into two categories. The first is that a student did not require special education services (codes 1-2) at any point in the dataset. The other category will be that they require special education (codes 4 - 9) at any point in the dataset.
Limited English: keep with no changes.
Primary language: I’m going to create two categories. One being that English is the primary language while the other will be that English is NOT the primary language at any point in the dataset.
Homeless: Keep with no changes.

We first need to create a list of all individuals with the StatusEnd code of 8 or 9 (graduated). In addition, we need to remove any student that graduated before 2008. This is because we will not have their full Sophmore - Senior records which is especially important when attempting to identify whether the number of CTE courses taken predicts migration.
The other element I want to add is a pre-covid and post-covid variable. All individuals whose grad year is before 2020 will be labeled pre-covid and all others wil lbe post-covid.
This “graduates list” will be the list on which we will bind all the other variables.


graduates <- enrollment.org.sw %>%
  filter(StatusEnd %in% c(8,9)) %>%
  mutate(grad.year = str_sub(TimeID, 1, 4),
         grad.year = as.integer(grad.year),
         grad.year.covid = ifelse(grad.year > 2019, "Post-covid grad", "Pre-covid grad")) %>%
  select(PersonID, K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, grad.year, grad.year.covid, Gender, LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator, HomelessIndicator, county.name, countyfp, Dem_Desc, edr, planning.region) %>%
  distinct(PersonID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(grad.year > 2007) 


Next we need to create new columns for the variables that will indicate a certain status has been met at any point in the dataset for each PersonID. For example, we want a column that indicates a PersonID was enrolled in PSEO at some point in the dataset. The variables that need a new column created are;

EconomicStatusIndicator
PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator & PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator
SpecialEdStatus
HomeprimaryLanguage



# Lets start with economic status indicator
k12.enr.esi.indicator <- enrollment.org.sw %>%
  select(PersonID, EconomicStatusIndicator) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  mutate(economic.status.code = ifelse(EconomicStatusIndicator %in% c(1,2,4,5), 1, 0),
         economic.status.code.sum = sum(economic.status.code)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(PersonID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(economic.status = ifelse(economic.status.code.sum > 0, 1, 0)) %>%
  select(PersonID, economic.status)

# PSEO

k12.enr.pseo.indicator <- enrollment.org.sw%>%
  select(PersonID, PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator, PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator) %>%
  mutate(PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator = ifelse(PostSecondaryEnrollmentOptionsIndicator == "Y", 1, 0),
         PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator = ifelse(PSEOConcurrentEnrollmentIndicator == "Y", 1, 0)) %>%
  gather(key = "pseo.type", value = "indicator", 2:3) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  summarise(pseo = sum(indicator)) %>%
  mutate(pseo.participant = ifelse(pseo > 0, 1, 0)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(PersonID, pseo.participant)

# Special Ed status

k12.enr.spced.indicator <- enrollment.org.sw %>%
  select(PersonID, SpecialEdStatus) %>%
  mutate(SpecialEdStatus.code = ifelse(SpecialEdStatus %in% c(4,5,6,7,8,9), 1, 0)) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  summarise(SpecialEdStatus.sum = sum(SpecialEdStatus.code)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(SpecialEdStatus = ifelse(SpecialEdStatus.sum > 0, 1, 0)) %>%
  select(PersonID, SpecialEdStatus)

# Home Primary Language

k12.enr.homeprimlang.indicator <- enrollment.org.sw %>%
  select(PersonID, HomePrimaryLanguage) %>%
  mutate(HomePrimaryLanguage.code = ifelse(HomePrimaryLanguage == 11, 0, 1)) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  summarise(HomePrimaryLanguage.sum = sum(HomePrimaryLanguage.code)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(non.english.home = ifelse(HomePrimaryLanguage.sum > 0, 1, 0)) %>%
  select(PersonID, non.english.home)

# Race

k12.enr.race <- enrollment.org.sw %>%
  select(PersonID, RaceEthnicity) %>%
  mutate(RaceEthnicity = ifelse(RaceEthnicity == 0, "Unknown",
                                ifelse(RaceEthnicity == 1, "AI",
                                       ifelse(RaceEthnicity == 2, "Asian/PI",
                                              ifelse(RaceEthnicity == 3, "Hispanic",
                                                     ifelse(RaceEthnicity == 4, "Black", "White"))))),
         RaceEthnicity = as.factor(RaceEthnicity)) %>%
  distinct(PersonID, RaceEthnicity) %>%
  mutate(code = ifelse(RaceEthnicity == "Unknown", 0, 1)) %>%
  group_by(PersonID) %>%
  filter(code == max(code)) %>%
  ungroup %>%
  distinct(PersonID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(PersonID, RaceEthnicity)


Now we can combine the list of graduates with the status indicators.


k12.enrollment.master <- graduates %>%
  left_join(k12.enr.esi.indicator, by = "PersonID") %>%
  left_join(k12.enr.pseo.indicator, by = "PersonID") %>%
  left_join(k12.enr.spced.indicator, by = "PersonID") %>%
  left_join(k12.enr.homeprimlang.indicator, by = "PersonID") %>%
  left_join(k12.enr.race, by = "PersonID") %>%
  select(PersonID, K12OrganizationID, OrganizationName, county.name, countyfp, Dem_Desc, edr, planning.region, grad.year, grad.year.covid, Gender, LimitedEnglishProficiencyIndicator, HomelessIndicator, economic.status, pseo.participant, SpecialEdStatus, non.english.home, RaceEthnicity)

kable(head(k12.enrollment.master))

The prepped enrollment data has 54,063 observations and 18 columns.



Summary of K12Enrollment data
Below is the percentage of total observations that;

Were eligible for free or reduced lunch at some point in the dataset
Are Female
Have been homeless at some point in the dataset
Had limited english proficiency at some point in the dataset
Had non english speakders in home at some point in the dataset
Participated in a PSEO course at some point in the dataset.
Pct non white

As expected, nearly half (49.2%) of the observations are female. Over a third (37%) of the observations have been eligible for free or reduced lunch at some point in the dataset. Also, over a third (33.4%) of observations participated in a PSEO course. There is a large percentage identifying as non-white with 34.1%, while 13.8% were categorized as needing special education, 10% with a non-english speaker in the home, 2.9% with limited english proficiency and 0.5% homeless.









write_csv(k12.enrollment.master, "Data/SLEDS/Masters/k12_enrollment.csv")
